content.content:
1→# Attlas Dashboard Feature Plan 2→ 3→Consolidated execution plan for 5 dashboard features. Every decision in 4→this document was locked through interactive planning — no open questions. 5→When executing, follow the order in the "Order of implementation" section. 6→ 7→## Locked decisions 8→ 9→| # | Feature | Scope | 10→|---|---|---| 11→| F0 | Tab title + favicon consistency | Hand-drawn Atlas-shrugging SVG favicon, `attlas` on every route where controllable, openclaw accepted as-is | 12→| F1 | Dotfiles sync panel | Backend reads systemctl + git, frontend shows timestamp + commit hash + `[sync now]` button | 13→| F2 | Openclaw detail page (simplified) | 3 cards: spend (month total + 7-day bar chart with hover), usage (telegram sessions + tasks), status (running + uptime + active tasks). Data via `openclaw status --all --json` subprocess + Anthropic billing API. No Brave. No sqlite file reads. | 14→| F3 | Domain expiry banner | `whois attlas.uk`, parsed, cached 6h, color-coded by days remaining | 15→| F6 | Dashboard redesign | Main dashboard = hero-driven minimal, detail pages = modern card-based, both in diary color palette | 16→ 17→**F5 (sqlite as a service) was dropped entirely. Do not implement.** 18→ 19→## Color palette (diary-derived) 20→ 21→Add these as CSS variables in `frontend/src/index.css`: 22→ 23→```css 24→:root { 25→ --bg: #0c0c0c; /* page background */ 26→ --fg: #c0c0c0; /* default body text */ 27→ --border: #333; /* dividers, section rules */ 28→ --accent: #00ff00; /* terminal green — all links, ok indicators */ 29→ --brand: #ff8700; /* orange — "attlas" brand, headline numbers */ 30→ --header: #ffff00; /* yellow — section labels like "── STATUS ──" */ 31→ --muted: #8a8a8a; /* timestamps, secondary info */ 32→ --surface: #1a1a1a; /* card backgrounds, input backgrounds */ 33→ --danger: #ff5555; /* error states */ 34→ --warning: #ffcc00; /* warning states */ 35→} 36→``` 37→ 38→## Typography 39→ 40→Load both from Google Fonts in `frontend/index.html`: 41→ 42→- **Inter** — primary UI font (sans-serif, modern, reads well at all sizes) 43→- **JetBrains Mono** — technical values only: IPs, commit hashes, paths, 44→ timestamps, code 45→ 46→## Cross-cutting work (do this before any feature) 47→ 48→### Frontend refactor 49→ 50→1. `cd frontend && npm install react-router-dom` 51→2. Split `frontend/src/App.jsx` into: 52→ - `App.jsx` — router + global layout (header, domain banner, logout, footer, 53→ `` for page content). 54→ - `pages/Dashboard.jsx` — home view. 55→ - `pages/detail/Openclaw.jsx` — openclaw detail view. 56→3. Extract shared UI primitives into `frontend/src/components/`: 57→ - `StatusDot.jsx` — colored dot (green/red/yellow/grey) 58→ - `Card.jsx` — `
` with `--surface` background, rounded, bordered 59→ - `Button.jsx` — primary / ghost / danger variants 60→ - `Banner.jsx` — domain expiry banner component 61→4. Add the CSS variables and font imports to `frontend/src/index.css` and 62→ `frontend/index.html`. 63→5. Route table: 64→ - `/` → `` 65→ - `/services/details/openclaw` → `` 66→ - All other routes handled server-side by alive-server (the router only 67→ owns the SPA routes). 68→ 69→### Backend refactor (alive-server `main.go`) 70→ 71→1. Add `getDotfilesStatus()`, `getDomainExpiry()`, `handleOpenclawDetail()` 72→ helper functions. 73→2. Add fields to `/api/status` response: 74→ - `dotfiles` — `{last_sync, head_commit, remote_commit, behind, status}` 75→ - `domain_expiry` — `{expires_at, days_remaining, severity}` 76→3. New endpoints: 77→ - `POST /api/dotfiles/sync` — triggers `systemctl start dotfiles-sync.service` 78→ - `GET /api/services/openclaw` — returns openclaw detail data 79→4. Extend `OAuthConfig` struct: 80→ ```go 81→ type OAuthConfig struct { 82→ ClientID string `json:"google_oauth_client_id"` 83→ ClientSecret string `json:"google_oauth_client_secret"` 84→ AllowedEmails []string `json:"allowed_emails"` 85→ AnthropicAdminKey string `json:"anthropic_admin_key"` // NEW 86→ } 87→ ``` 88→5. Add `whois` parser helper — shells out to `whois attlas.uk`, greps for 89→ `Expiry date:`, parses to ISO8601. Cache for 6 hours. 90→ 91→### New sudoers drop-ins 92→ 93→Add these to `base-setup/setup.sh`. Each must be validated with 94→`visudo -c -f ` after writing. 95→ 96→**`/etc/sudoers.d/alive-svc-openclaw`** (for F2): 97→``` 98→Defaults:alive-svc env_keep += "HOME" 99→alive-svc ALL=(openclaw-svc) NOPASSWD: /usr/bin/openclaw 100→``` 101→ 102→**`/etc/sudoers.d/alive-svc-dotfiles-sync`** (for F1 `[sync now]` button): 103→``` 104→alive-svc ALL=(root) NOPASSWD: /bin/systemctl start dotfiles-sync.service 105→``` 106→ 107→### Caddyfile changes (F0) 108→ 109→Add a global favicon handler in `base-setup/Caddyfile` so every subpath 110→falls through to the same SVG icon. Placement: before the per-service 111→imports, so it takes priority over upstream `/favicon.ico` paths. 112→ 113→```caddy 114→{$CADDY_DOMAIN} { 115→ # Global favicon override — every */favicon.{ico,svg,png} request 116→ # returns the attlas SVG regardless of subpath. 117→ @favicon path_regexp ^/.*favicon\.(ico|svg|png)$ 118→ handle @favicon { 119→ header Content-Type "image/svg+xml" 120→ header Cache-Control "public, max-age=86400" 121→ rewrite * /favicon.svg 122→ reverse_proxy localhost:3000 123→ } 124→ 125→ # ... existing forward_auth and imports ... 126→} 127→``` 128→ 129→Note: the rewrite + reverse_proxy lets alive-server serve the SVG from 130→its frontend `public/` directory. Alternative is a `respond` with the 131→SVG inlined into the Caddyfile — pick whichever is more maintainable. 132→ 133→### Package additions (add to `base-setup/setup.sh` apt-get line) 134→ 135→- `whois` — for F3 136→ 137→### Cleanup from earlier discovery 138→ 139→The `sqlite3` apt package was installed on the VM during discovery when 140→inspecting openclaw's database. F5 is dropped and nothing else uses it: 141→ 142→```bash 143→sudo apt remove --purge -y sqlite3 144→``` 145→ 146→Run this as part of the first commit's deployment, so the box is clean. 147→ 148→## Feature specs 149→ 150→### F0 — Tab title + favicon 151→ 152→**Favicon**: hand-drawn SVG of Atlas with the celestial sphere floating 153→above his visibly-not-holding-it hands. Palette: `--brand` (#ff8700) for 154→the figure, `--accent` (#00ff00) highlights for the sphere. Save as 155→`frontend/public/favicon.svg`. 156→ 157→**Dashboard ``**: edit `frontend/index.html`: 158→```html 159→<title>attlas 160→ 161→``` 162→ 163→**Per-upstream tab titles** (4-of-5 consistency — openclaw accepted as-is): 164→- **Dashboard** → `attlas` in `frontend/index.html` 165→- **ttyd** → add `--title-format=attlas` to `ExecStart` in `services/install-terminal.sh` 166→- **code-server** → add `--app-name=attlas` to `ExecStart` in `services/install-code-server.sh` 167→- **Hugo diary** → edit `diary/layouts/_default/baseof.html` `` block: 168→ ```html 169→ <title>{{ if .IsHome }}attlas{{ else }}attlas — {{ .Title }}{{ end }} 170→ ``` 171→- **openclaw** → accepted as-is (its UI hardcodes the title in bundled JS) 172→ 173→**Cross-route favicon**: via the Caddy global handler in the "Caddyfile 174→changes" section above. Every `/*/favicon.*` request at any subpath 175→returns the same SVG. 176→ 177→### F1 — Dotfiles sync panel 178→ 179→**Backend helpers** in `main.go`: 180→ 181→```go 182→type DotfilesStatus struct { 183→ LastSync string `json:"last_sync"` // ISO timestamp 184→ LastExitStatus int `json:"last_exit_status"` // 0 = ok 185→ HeadCommit string `json:"head_commit"` // short hash 186→ HeadCommittedAt string `json:"head_committed_at"` // ISO 187→ RemoteCommit string `json:"remote_commit"` // short hash 188→ Behind int `json:"behind"` // commit count 189→ Status string `json:"status"` // "up-to-date" | "behind" | "error" 190→} 191→ 192→func getDotfilesStatus() DotfilesStatus { 193→ // systemctl show dotfiles-sync.service -p ExecMainExitTimestamp -p ExecMainStatus 194→ // git -C /home/agnostic-user/iapetus/dotfiels rev-parse HEAD 195→ // git -C ... rev-parse origin/master 196→ // git -C ... log -1 --format=%cI HEAD 197→ // git -C ... rev-list --count HEAD..origin/master 198→} 199→``` 200→ 201→Wired into `/api/status` as a new `dotfiles` field. 202→ 203→**New endpoint**: `POST /api/dotfiles/sync` 204→```go 205→func handleDotfilesSync(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) { 206→ cmd := exec.Command("sudo", "-n", "systemctl", "start", "dotfiles-sync.service") 207→ if err := cmd.Run(); err != nil { 208→ sendJSON(w, map[string]interface{}{"error": err.Error()}) 209→ return 210→ } 211→ sendJSON(w, map[string]interface{}{"success": true}) 212→} 213→``` 214→ 215→**Frontend**: new row inside the STATUS section of `pages/Dashboard.jsx`: 216→``` 217→dotfiles ● up to date · 8c3ea15 · synced 2 min ago sync now 218→``` 219→- Green dot if status=up-to-date and last_exit=0 220→- Yellow dot if behind 221→- Red dot if last_exit != 0 222→- `[sync now]` is a text link (uses `--accent` color) that calls `POST /api/dotfiles/sync` and refreshes status on success. 223→ 224→### F2 — Openclaw detail page 225→ 226→**Route**: `/services/details/openclaw` 227→ 228→**Backend endpoint**: `GET /api/services/openclaw` 229→ 230→```go 231→type OpenclawDetail struct { 232→ Running bool `json:"running"` 233→ Uptime string `json:"uptime"` // human-readable 234→ ActiveTasks int `json:"active_tasks"` 235→ TelegramSessions int `json:"telegram_sessions"` // last 7 days 236→ TasksRun int `json:"tasks_run"` // last 7 days 237→ SpendThisMonth float64 `json:"spend_this_month"` // USD 238→ SpendDaily []DayCost `json:"spend_daily"` // last 7 days 239→} 240→ 241→type DayCost struct { 242→ Date string `json:"date"` // "2026-04-10" 243→ USD float64 `json:"usd"` 244→} 245→ 246→func handleOpenclawDetail(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) { 247→ // 1. systemctl show openclaw-gateway — uptime, running state 248→ // 2. sudo -n -u openclaw-svc -H /usr/bin/openclaw status --all --json 249→ // Parse the JSON (shape TBD — inspect on the VM first). 250→ // Extract: active tasks, telegram session count, tasks run count. 251→ // 3. Anthropic cost_report API call: 252→ // GET https://api.anthropic.com/v1/organizations/cost_report 253→ // ?starting_at= 254→ // &bucket_width=1d 255→ // Parse buckets into spend_this_month and spend_daily (last 7). 256→ // Cache 30s. 257→} 258→``` 259→ 260→**Frontend** (`pages/detail/Openclaw.jsx`): 261→ 262→``` 263→back to dashboard ← 264→ 265→openclaw 266→AI agent daemon 267→ 268→┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ 269→│ SPEND │ 270→│ │ 271→│ $42.18 │ 272→│ april │ 273→│ │ 274→│ [7-day SVG bar chart — see below] │ 275→└──────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ 276→ 277→┌───────────────────┐ ┌──────────────────────────┐ 278→│ USAGE · 7 DAYS │ │ STATUS │ 279→│ │ │ │ 280→│ 14 telegram │ │ ● running │ 281→│ sessions │ │ uptime 3d 14h │ 282→│ 47 tasks run │ │ active 2 tasks │ 283→└───────────────────┘ └──────────────────────────┘ 284→ 285→ open openclaw ↗ 286→``` 287→ 288→**Bar chart implementation** (inline SVG, no library): 289→- 7 bars representing the last 7 days 290→- Each bar is a `` with height proportional to USD 291→- `` child on each `<rect>` = native browser tooltip showing `$X.XX on <date>` 292→- Day labels below the bars using `<text>` elements 293→- Bars colored `--accent` (green), tallest bar or "today" colored `--brand` (orange) — pick on implementation 294→- ~30 lines of React 295→ 296→**Visual spec for the detail page chrome**: 297→- Modern card layout: cards have `background: var(--surface)`, `border: 1px solid var(--border)`, `border-radius: 8px`, generous padding 298→- Big headline number in `--brand` orange, large font 299→- Labels in `--muted` grey, small caps 300→- `● running` uses `--accent` green for the dot 301→- Header has `← back to dashboard` link (uses `--accent` color, hover underline) and the same logout link as the main dashboard 302→ 303→**Known unknown**: the exact JSON shape of `openclaw status --all --json` is 304→not confirmed. Before writing the Go struct, run this on the VM: 305→```bash 306→sudo -u openclaw-svc -H /usr/bin/openclaw status --all --json | python3 -m json.tool | head -100 307→``` 308→and map the keys to the `OpenclawDetail` fields. If openclaw doesn't expose 309→`telegram_sessions` or `active_tasks` directly, fall back to: 310→- Telegram sessions: grep for any telegram-related session keys in the 311→ JSON, or count rows in `runs.sqlite` with a telegram label. If neither 312→ works, remove the field from the usage card and ship with only 313→ `tasks_run`. **Do not silently ship a broken card — either find a source 314→ or remove the field.** 315→- Active tasks: if not in status JSON, count task_runs with 316→ `status='running'` in runs.sqlite. Requires the sqlite dependency back 317→ — only add it if actually needed. 318→ 319→### F3 — Domain expiry banner 320→ 321→**Backend helper** in `main.go`: 322→```go 323→type DomainExpiry struct { 324→ ExpiresAt string `json:"expires_at"` // ISO date 325→ DaysRemaining int `json:"days_remaining"` 326→ Severity string `json:"severity"` // ok | warn | danger 327→} 328→ 329→func getDomainExpiry() DomainExpiry { 330→ // shell out: whois attlas.uk 331→ // grep for "Expiry date:" 332→ // parse "09-Apr-2027" → ISO → days from now 333→ // severity: "ok" if >60d, "warn" if 30-60d, "danger" if <30d 334→ // cache 6 hours 335→} 336→``` 337→ 338→Wired into `/api/status` as `domain_expiry`. 339→ 340→**Frontend banner component** in `App.jsx` (shows on every route): 341→```jsx 342→function DomainBanner({ expiry }) { 343→ if (!expiry || expiry.severity === "ok") return null; // hide when far 344→ // yellow border/accent for warn 345→ // red for danger 346→ return ( 347→ <div className={`banner banner-${expiry.severity}`}> 348→ ⚠ domain attlas.uk expires in {expiry.days_remaining} days 349→ ({expiry.expires_at}) 350→ <a href="https://dash.cloudflare.com">renew</a> 351→ </div> 352→ ); 353→} 354→``` 355→ 356→**Package**: add `whois` to the apt-get install line in `base-setup/setup.sh`. 357→ 358→### F6 — Dashboard redesign 359→ 360→**Main dashboard** (`pages/Dashboard.jsx`) — hero-driven minimal: 361→ 362→``` 363→┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ 364→│ │ 365→│ attlas you@email logout │ 366→│ │ 367→│ │ 368→│ ⬤ all systems healthy │ 369→│ all 5 services running, dotfiles up to date │ 370→│ │ 371→│ │ 372→│ ──── STATUS ─────────────────────────────────────────────── │ 373→│ │ 374→│ vm simple-zombie · europe-west1-b │ 375→│ ip 34.62.185.156 │ 376→│ domain attlas.uk → renews in 365 days │ 377→│ claude ● authenticated logout │ 378→│ dotfiles ● up to date · 2 min ago sync now │ 379→│ │ 380→│ │ 381→│ ──── SERVICES ───────────────────────────────────────────── │ 382→│ │ 383→│ ⬤ cloud terminal /terminal open details × │ 384→│ ⬤ cloud vs code /code open details × │ 385→│ ⬤ openclaw /openclaw open details × │ 386→│ ⬤ project diary /diary/ open details × │ 387→│ │ 388→└────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ 389→``` 390→ 391→**Key UX moves:** 392→- **Hero status** at top: big colored dot + one-line phrase. Green "all systems healthy" when nothing is wrong; yellow/red with count and brief description when anything is off. 393→- **Single status section** folds VM info + domain + claude + dotfiles into aligned rows. No more three separate sections. 394→- **Service rows** get `open` and `details` text links — biggest current omission. `open` goes to the service's URL, `details` goes to `/services/details/<id>`. Uninstall is a small `×` at the row end. 395→- **`[sync now]` on dotfiles row** triggers F1's new endpoint. 396→- **Only the openclaw row has a working `details` link in the first cut**. Other services' details links can be disabled (greyed out) or point to a "not yet implemented" page. 397→ 398→**Layout**: 399→- Single column, `max-width: 720px`, centered, generous vertical padding 400→- Inter for text, JetBrains Mono for IPs/hashes/timestamps/paths 401→- Section labels (`STATUS`, `SERVICES`) in `--header` yellow, with a thin `--border` rule 402→ 403→**Detail pages** (`pages/detail/Openclaw.jsx`) — modern card-based: 404→- Header: `← back to dashboard` link + page title + subtitle 405→- Body: grid of cards (2-column on desktop, 1-column on mobile) 406→- Cards: `--surface` background, rounded, `--border` stroke, generous padding 407→- Headline metric in `--brand` orange, large 408→- Action row at the bottom: primary action as a proper `<button>` (green bg, black text) not a text link 409→- Footer: same domain banner + logout as the main dashboard 410→ 411→**Shared chrome (both dashboard and detail pages)**: 412→- Domain banner above the header when `severity !== "ok"` (F3) 413→- Toast notifications bottom-right: green success, red error, yellow warning, auto-dismiss 4s 414→- Loading state: pulsing `--accent` dot, not a spinner 415→- Focus states: bright `--accent` outline for keyboard navigation 416→- Claude login modal stays functional, restyled to match 417→ 418→## Files that will change 419→ 420→**Frontend** (`base-setup/alive-server/frontend/`): 421→- `index.html` — title, favicon link, font imports 422→- `src/index.css` — CSS variables, new components styles 423→- `src/App.jsx` — refactored to router + layout 424→- `src/pages/Dashboard.jsx` — new (extracted from old App.jsx, redesigned) 425→- `src/pages/detail/Openclaw.jsx` — new 426→- `src/components/StatusDot.jsx` — new 427→- `src/components/Card.jsx` — new 428→- `src/components/Button.jsx` — new 429→- `src/components/Banner.jsx` — new 430→- `public/favicon.svg` — new (hand-drawn) 431→- `package.json` — add `react-router-dom` 432→ 433→**Backend** (`base-setup/alive-server/`): 434→- `main.go` — `getDotfilesStatus()`, `getDomainExpiry()`, `handleOpenclawDetail()`, `handleDotfilesSync()`, extended OAuthConfig struct, new routes 435→- `go.mod` — no new dependencies (stretch: may add `modernc.org/sqlite` only if F2 fallback requires it) 436→ 437→**Setup scripts**: 438→- `base-setup/setup.sh` — add whois to apt list, add new sudoers drop-ins, everything else unchanged 439→- `base-setup/Caddyfile` — global favicon handler 440→- `services/install-terminal.sh` — `--title-format=attlas` 441→- `services/install-code-server.sh` — `--app-name=attlas` 442→ 443→**Diary template**: 444→- `diary/layouts/_default/baseof.html` — `<title>` block update 445→ 446→## Order of implementation 447→ 448→Execute sequentially. Commit after each step. Push after each clean build. 449→ 450→1. **Pre-work** (frontend refactor): install `react-router-dom`, split `App.jsx` 451→ into router + pages, add CSS variables + fonts, create shared components. 452→ Build must pass before moving on. 453→2. **F0**: favicon SVG + index.html title + Caddy global handler + 454→ per-upstream title flags. Verify on the VM: open `/terminal`, `/code`, 455→ `/diary` and confirm the tab icon is the attlas SVG on all three. 456→3. **F6 (shells)**: redesigned main dashboard + detail page scaffolding 457→ with the new card/hero chrome, using placeholder data. This lets the 458→ other features slot into a finished-looking layout. 459→4. **F1**: dotfiles panel + sync button + backend helpers + sudoers drop-in. 460→5. **F3**: domain expiry banner + whois install + cached helper. 461→6. **F2**: openclaw detail page. **First step: run `openclaw status --all 462→ --json` on the VM and document the actual JSON shape** before writing 463→ the Go struct. Then backend endpoint, then frontend card rendering with 464→ the SVG bar chart. 465→7. **Cleanup**: `apt remove --purge sqlite3` on the VM. 466→ 467→Estimated total: ~4.5 hours of focused work. 468→ 469→## Known risks to resolve during implementation 470→ 471→- **F2 openclaw status JSON shape is unverified**. Step 1 of F2 is to run 472→ the command and inspect output. If `telegram_sessions` isn't exposed, 473→ use the fallback plan documented in the F2 section. 474→- **Caddy favicon rewrite** may conflict with existing path handlers. 475→ Test `/favicon.ico`, `/terminal/favicon.ico`, `/code/favicon.ico` after 476→ deploying — all three should return the attlas SVG. If not, adjust 477→ the matcher precedence. 478→- **`openclaw status` subprocess may hang** if the gateway is unresponsive. 479→ Wrap the call in a 2-second timeout (`exec.CommandContext` with 480→ `context.WithTimeout`). On timeout, return last-known-good cached data 481→ or a "temporarily unavailable" state — never block the dashboard. 482→- **Anthropic cost_report API** may return 401 if the admin key is revoked 483→ or the billing endpoint is disabled for the org. Handle 4xx responses 484→ gracefully and show "billing data unavailable" in the spend card 485→ instead of crashing. 486→- **`whois` package** may not be installed on a fresh VM before the first 487→ run of base-setup. The domain banner backend call must tolerate 488→ `whois: command not found` and return "unknown" severity. 489→ 490→## Deployment 491→ 492→After every feature commit on the local laptop: 493→ 494→1. Build the frontend: `cd frontend && npm run build` 495→2. Commit both the source AND the `dist/` changes to the attlas repo. 496→3. Push to origin/main. 497→4. SSH to the VM, pull the attlas repo as `agnostic-user`, rebuild 498→ alive-server as `agnostic-user`, restart the service: 499→ ```bash 500→ sudo -u agnostic-user bash -c "cd /home/agnostic-user/iapetus/attlas && git pull" 501→ sudo -u agnostic-user bash -c "cd /home/agnostic-user/iapetus/attlas/base-setup/alive-server && PATH=\$PATH:/usr/local/go/bin go build -o attlas-server ." 502→ sudo systemctl restart alive-server 503→ ``` 504→5. For changes that touch Caddy or sudoers: additionally run 505→ `sudo systemctl reload caddy` and validate sudoers with 506→ `sudo visudo -c`. 507→6. Verify via the dashboard URL that the change is live. 508→ 509→## Out of scope (explicitly, do not add) 510→ 511→- Brave search metrics (no API, no on-disk data). 512→- Sqlite as a service (F5 was dropped). 513→- Token counts on the openclaw spend card (dropped by user decision). 514→- Average-cost-per-day footer on the spend card (dropped by user decision). 515→- Provider quota card on the openclaw detail page (dropped in favor of 516→ the simpler 3-card layout). 517→- Detail pages for services other than openclaw in this pass. Other 518→ services' `details` links can be disabled or point to a stub. 519→- Reverse-proxy refactor to route all services through alive-server. 520→- Service install/uninstall sudoers scope widening. 521→- Multi-VM Terraform module refactor. 522→

# Attlas Dashboard Feature Plan Consolidated execution plan for 5 dashboard features. Every decision in this document was locked through interactive planning — no open questions. When executing, follow the order in the "Order of implementation" section. ## Locked decisions | # | Feature | Scope | |---|---|---| | F0 | Tab title + favicon consistency | Hand-drawn Atlas-shrugging SVG favicon, `attlas` on every route where controllable, openclaw accepted as-is | | F1 | Dotfiles sync panel | Backend reads systemctl + git, frontend shows timestamp + commit hash + `[sync now]` button | | F2 | Openclaw detail page (simplified) | 3 cards: spend (month total + 7-day bar chart with hover), usage (telegram sessions + tasks), status (running + uptime + active tasks). Data via `openclaw status --all --json` subprocess + Anthropic billing API. No Brave. No sqlite file reads. | | F3 | Domain expiry banner | `whois attlas.uk`, parsed, cached 6h, color-coded by days remaining | | F6 | Dashboard redesign | Main dashboard = hero-driven minimal, detail pages = modern card-based, both in diary color palette | **F5 (sqlite as a service) was dropped entirely. Do not implement.** ## Color palette (diary-derived) Add these as CSS variables in `frontend/src/index.css`: ```css :root { --bg: #0c0c0c; /* page background */ --fg: #c0c0c0; /* default body text */ --border: #333; /* dividers, section rules */ --accent: #00ff00; /* terminal green — all links, ok indicators */ --brand: #ff8700; /* orange — "attlas" brand, headline numbers */ --header: #ffff00; /* yellow — section labels like "── STATUS ──" */ --muted: #8a8a8a; /* timestamps, secondary info */ --surface: #1a1a1a; /* card backgrounds, input backgrounds */ --danger: #ff5555; /* error states */ --warning: #ffcc00; /* warning states */ } ``` ## Typography Load both from Google Fonts in `frontend/index.html`: - **Inter** — primary UI font (sans-serif, modern, reads well at all sizes) - **JetBrains Mono** — technical values only: IPs, commit hashes, paths, timestamps, code ## Cross-cutting work (do this before any feature) ### Frontend refactor 1. `cd frontend && npm install react-router-dom` 2. Split `frontend/src/App.jsx` into: - `App.jsx` — router + global layout (header, domain banner, logout, footer, `` for page content). - `pages/Dashboard.jsx` — home view. - `pages/detail/Openclaw.jsx` — openclaw detail view. 3. Extract shared UI primitives into `frontend/src/components/`: - `StatusDot.jsx` — colored dot (green/red/yellow/grey) - `Card.jsx` — `
` with `--surface` background, rounded, bordered - `Button.jsx` — primary / ghost / danger variants - `Banner.jsx` — domain expiry banner component 4. Add the CSS variables and font imports to `frontend/src/index.css` and `frontend/index.html`. 5. Route table: - `/` → `` - `/services/details/openclaw` → `` - All other routes handled server-side by alive-server (the router only owns the SPA routes). ### Backend refactor (alive-server `main.go`) 1. Add `getDotfilesStatus()`, `getDomainExpiry()`, `handleOpenclawDetail()` helper functions. 2. Add fields to `/api/status` response: - `dotfiles` — `{last_sync, head_commit, remote_commit, behind, status}` - `domain_expiry` — `{expires_at, days_remaining, severity}` 3. New endpoints: - `POST /api/dotfiles/sync` — triggers `systemctl start dotfiles-sync.service` - `GET /api/services/openclaw` — returns openclaw detail data 4. Extend `OAuthConfig` struct: ```go type OAuthConfig struct { ClientID string `json:"google_oauth_client_id"` ClientSecret string `json:"google_oauth_client_secret"` AllowedEmails []string `json:"allowed_emails"` AnthropicAdminKey string `json:"anthropic_admin_key"` // NEW } ``` 5. Add `whois` parser helper — shells out to `whois attlas.uk`, greps for `Expiry date:`, parses to ISO8601. Cache for 6 hours. ### New sudoers drop-ins Add these to `base-setup/setup.sh`. Each must be validated with `visudo -c -f ` after writing. **`/etc/sudoers.d/alive-svc-openclaw`** (for F2): ``` Defaults:alive-svc env_keep += "HOME" alive-svc ALL=(openclaw-svc) NOPASSWD: /usr/bin/openclaw ``` **`/etc/sudoers.d/alive-svc-dotfiles-sync`** (for F1 `[sync now]` button): ``` alive-svc ALL=(root) NOPASSWD: /bin/systemctl start dotfiles-sync.service ``` ### Caddyfile changes (F0) Add a global favicon handler in `base-setup/Caddyfile` so every subpath falls through to the same SVG icon. Placement: before the per-service imports, so it takes priority over upstream `/favicon.ico` paths. ```caddy {$CADDY_DOMAIN} { # Global favicon override — every */favicon.{ico,svg,png} request # returns the attlas SVG regardless of subpath. @favicon path_regexp ^/.*favicon\.(ico|svg|png)$ handle @favicon { header Content-Type "image/svg+xml" header Cache-Control "public, max-age=86400" rewrite * /favicon.svg reverse_proxy localhost:3000 } # ... existing forward_auth and imports ... } ``` Note: the rewrite + reverse_proxy lets alive-server serve the SVG from its frontend `public/` directory. Alternative is a `respond` with the SVG inlined into the Caddyfile — pick whichever is more maintainable. ### Package additions (add to `base-setup/setup.sh` apt-get line) - `whois` — for F3 ### Cleanup from earlier discovery The `sqlite3` apt package was installed on the VM during discovery when inspecting openclaw's database. F5 is dropped and nothing else uses it: ```bash sudo apt remove --purge -y sqlite3 ``` Run this as part of the first commit's deployment, so the box is clean. ## Feature specs ### F0 — Tab title + favicon **Favicon**: hand-drawn SVG of Atlas with the celestial sphere floating above his visibly-not-holding-it hands. Palette: `--brand` (#ff8700) for the figure, `--accent` (#00ff00) highlights for the sphere. Save as `frontend/public/favicon.svg`. **Dashboard ``**: edit `frontend/index.html`: ```html <title>attlas ``` **Per-upstream tab titles** (4-of-5 consistency — openclaw accepted as-is): - **Dashboard** → `attlas` in `frontend/index.html` - **ttyd** → add `--title-format=attlas` to `ExecStart` in `services/install-terminal.sh` - **code-server** → add `--app-name=attlas` to `ExecStart` in `services/install-code-server.sh` - **Hugo diary** → edit `diary/layouts/_default/baseof.html` `` block: ```html <title>{{ if .IsHome }}attlas{{ else }}attlas — {{ .Title }}{{ end }} ``` - **openclaw** → accepted as-is (its UI hardcodes the title in bundled JS) **Cross-route favicon**: via the Caddy global handler in the "Caddyfile changes" section above. Every `/*/favicon.*` request at any subpath returns the same SVG. ### F1 — Dotfiles sync panel **Backend helpers** in `main.go`: ```go type DotfilesStatus struct { LastSync string `json:"last_sync"` // ISO timestamp LastExitStatus int `json:"last_exit_status"` // 0 = ok HeadCommit string `json:"head_commit"` // short hash HeadCommittedAt string `json:"head_committed_at"` // ISO RemoteCommit string `json:"remote_commit"` // short hash Behind int `json:"behind"` // commit count Status string `json:"status"` // "up-to-date" | "behind" | "error" } func getDotfilesStatus() DotfilesStatus { // systemctl show dotfiles-sync.service -p ExecMainExitTimestamp -p ExecMainStatus // git -C /home/agnostic-user/iapetus/dotfiels rev-parse HEAD // git -C ... rev-parse origin/master // git -C ... log -1 --format=%cI HEAD // git -C ... rev-list --count HEAD..origin/master } ``` Wired into `/api/status` as a new `dotfiles` field. **New endpoint**: `POST /api/dotfiles/sync` ```go func handleDotfilesSync(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) { cmd := exec.Command("sudo", "-n", "systemctl", "start", "dotfiles-sync.service") if err := cmd.Run(); err != nil { sendJSON(w, map[string]interface{}{"error": err.Error()}) return } sendJSON(w, map[string]interface{}{"success": true}) } ``` **Frontend**: new row inside the STATUS section of `pages/Dashboard.jsx`: ``` dotfiles ● up to date · 8c3ea15 · synced 2 min ago sync now ``` - Green dot if status=up-to-date and last_exit=0 - Yellow dot if behind - Red dot if last_exit != 0 - `[sync now]` is a text link (uses `--accent` color) that calls `POST /api/dotfiles/sync` and refreshes status on success. ### F2 — Openclaw detail page **Route**: `/services/details/openclaw` **Backend endpoint**: `GET /api/services/openclaw` ```go type OpenclawDetail struct { Running bool `json:"running"` Uptime string `json:"uptime"` // human-readable ActiveTasks int `json:"active_tasks"` TelegramSessions int `json:"telegram_sessions"` // last 7 days TasksRun int `json:"tasks_run"` // last 7 days SpendThisMonth float64 `json:"spend_this_month"` // USD SpendDaily []DayCost `json:"spend_daily"` // last 7 days } type DayCost struct { Date string `json:"date"` // "2026-04-10" USD float64 `json:"usd"` } func handleOpenclawDetail(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) { // 1. systemctl show openclaw-gateway — uptime, running state // 2. sudo -n -u openclaw-svc -H /usr/bin/openclaw status --all --json // Parse the JSON (shape TBD — inspect on the VM first). // Extract: active tasks, telegram session count, tasks run count. // 3. Anthropic cost_report API call: // GET https://api.anthropic.com/v1/organizations/cost_report // ?starting_at= // &bucket_width=1d // Parse buckets into spend_this_month and spend_daily (last 7). // Cache 30s. } ``` **Frontend** (`pages/detail/Openclaw.jsx`): ``` back to dashboard ← openclaw AI agent daemon ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │ SPEND │ │ │ │ $42.18 │ │ april │ │ │ │ [7-day SVG bar chart — see below] │ └──────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ ┌───────────────────┐ ┌──────────────────────────┐ │ USAGE · 7 DAYS │ │ STATUS │ │ │ │ │ │ 14 telegram │ │ ● running │ │ sessions │ │ uptime 3d 14h │ │ 47 tasks run │ │ active 2 tasks │ └───────────────────┘ └──────────────────────────┘ open openclaw ↗ ``` **Bar chart implementation** (inline SVG, no library): - 7 bars representing the last 7 days - Each bar is a `` with height proportional to USD - `` child on each `<rect>` = native browser tooltip showing `$X.XX on <date>` - Day labels below the bars using `<text>` elements - Bars colored `--accent` (green), tallest bar or "today" colored `--brand` (orange) — pick on implementation - ~30 lines of React **Visual spec for the detail page chrome**: - Modern card layout: cards have `background: var(--surface)`, `border: 1px solid var(--border)`, `border-radius: 8px`, generous padding - Big headline number in `--brand` orange, large font - Labels in `--muted` grey, small caps - `● running` uses `--accent` green for the dot - Header has `← back to dashboard` link (uses `--accent` color, hover underline) and the same logout link as the main dashboard **Known unknown**: the exact JSON shape of `openclaw status --all --json` is not confirmed. Before writing the Go struct, run this on the VM: ```bash sudo -u openclaw-svc -H /usr/bin/openclaw status --all --json | python3 -m json.tool | head -100 ``` and map the keys to the `OpenclawDetail` fields. If openclaw doesn't expose `telegram_sessions` or `active_tasks` directly, fall back to: - Telegram sessions: grep for any telegram-related session keys in the JSON, or count rows in `runs.sqlite` with a telegram label. If neither works, remove the field from the usage card and ship with only `tasks_run`. **Do not silently ship a broken card — either find a source or remove the field.** - Active tasks: if not in status JSON, count task_runs with `status='running'` in runs.sqlite. Requires the sqlite dependency back — only add it if actually needed. ### F3 — Domain expiry banner **Backend helper** in `main.go`: ```go type DomainExpiry struct { ExpiresAt string `json:"expires_at"` // ISO date DaysRemaining int `json:"days_remaining"` Severity string `json:"severity"` // ok | warn | danger } func getDomainExpiry() DomainExpiry { // shell out: whois attlas.uk // grep for "Expiry date:" // parse "09-Apr-2027" → ISO → days from now // severity: "ok" if >60d, "warn" if 30-60d, "danger" if <30d // cache 6 hours } ``` Wired into `/api/status` as `domain_expiry`. **Frontend banner component** in `App.jsx` (shows on every route): ```jsx function DomainBanner({ expiry }) { if (!expiry || expiry.severity === "ok") return null; // hide when far // yellow border/accent for warn // red for danger return ( <div className={`banner banner-${expiry.severity}`}> ⚠ domain attlas.uk expires in {expiry.days_remaining} days ({expiry.expires_at}) <a href="https://dash.cloudflare.com">renew</a> </div> ); } ``` **Package**: add `whois` to the apt-get install line in `base-setup/setup.sh`. ### F6 — Dashboard redesign **Main dashboard** (`pages/Dashboard.jsx`) — hero-driven minimal: ``` ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │ │ │ attlas you@email logout │ │ │ │ │ │ ⬤ all systems healthy │ │ all 5 services running, dotfiles up to date │ │ │ │ │ │ ──── STATUS ─────────────────────────────────────────────── │ │ │ │ vm simple-zombie · europe-west1-b │ │ ip 34.62.185.156 │ │ domain attlas.uk → renews in 365 days │ │ claude ● authenticated logout │ │ dotfiles ● up to date · 2 min ago sync now │ │ │ │ │ │ ──── SERVICES ───────────────────────────────────────────── │ │ │ │ ⬤ cloud terminal /terminal open details × │ │ ⬤ cloud vs code /code open details × │ │ ⬤ openclaw /openclaw open details × │ │ ⬤ project diary /diary/ open details × │ │ │ └────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ ``` **Key UX moves:** - **Hero status** at top: big colored dot + one-line phrase. Green "all systems healthy" when nothing is wrong; yellow/red with count and brief description when anything is off. - **Single status section** folds VM info + domain + claude + dotfiles into aligned rows. No more three separate sections. - **Service rows** get `open` and `details` text links — biggest current omission. `open` goes to the service's URL, `details` goes to `/services/details/<id>`. Uninstall is a small `×` at the row end. - **`[sync now]` on dotfiles row** triggers F1's new endpoint. - **Only the openclaw row has a working `details` link in the first cut**. Other services' details links can be disabled (greyed out) or point to a "not yet implemented" page. **Layout**: - Single column, `max-width: 720px`, centered, generous vertical padding - Inter for text, JetBrains Mono for IPs/hashes/timestamps/paths - Section labels (`STATUS`, `SERVICES`) in `--header` yellow, with a thin `--border` rule **Detail pages** (`pages/detail/Openclaw.jsx`) — modern card-based: - Header: `← back to dashboard` link + page title + subtitle - Body: grid of cards (2-column on desktop, 1-column on mobile) - Cards: `--surface` background, rounded, `--border` stroke, generous padding - Headline metric in `--brand` orange, large - Action row at the bottom: primary action as a proper `<button>` (green bg, black text) not a text link - Footer: same domain banner + logout as the main dashboard **Shared chrome (both dashboard and detail pages)**: - Domain banner above the header when `severity !== "ok"` (F3) - Toast notifications bottom-right: green success, red error, yellow warning, auto-dismiss 4s - Loading state: pulsing `--accent` dot, not a spinner - Focus states: bright `--accent` outline for keyboard navigation - Claude login modal stays functional, restyled to match ## Files that will change **Frontend** (`base-setup/alive-server/frontend/`): - `index.html` — title, favicon link, font imports - `src/index.css` — CSS variables, new components styles - `src/App.jsx` — refactored to router + layout - `src/pages/Dashboard.jsx` — new (extracted from old App.jsx, redesigned) - `src/pages/detail/Openclaw.jsx` — new - `src/components/StatusDot.jsx` — new - `src/components/Card.jsx` — new - `src/components/Button.jsx` — new - `src/components/Banner.jsx` — new - `public/favicon.svg` — new (hand-drawn) - `package.json` — add `react-router-dom` **Backend** (`base-setup/alive-server/`): - `main.go` — `getDotfilesStatus()`, `getDomainExpiry()`, `handleOpenclawDetail()`, `handleDotfilesSync()`, extended OAuthConfig struct, new routes - `go.mod` — no new dependencies (stretch: may add `modernc.org/sqlite` only if F2 fallback requires it) **Setup scripts**: - `base-setup/setup.sh` — add whois to apt list, add new sudoers drop-ins, everything else unchanged - `base-setup/Caddyfile` — global favicon handler - `services/install-terminal.sh` — `--title-format=attlas` - `services/install-code-server.sh` — `--app-name=attlas` **Diary template**: - `diary/layouts/_default/baseof.html` — `<title>` block update ## Order of implementation Execute sequentially. Commit after each step. Push after each clean build. 1. **Pre-work** (frontend refactor): install `react-router-dom`, split `App.jsx` into router + pages, add CSS variables + fonts, create shared components. Build must pass before moving on. 2. **F0**: favicon SVG + index.html title + Caddy global handler + per-upstream title flags. Verify on the VM: open `/terminal`, `/code`, `/diary` and confirm the tab icon is the attlas SVG on all three. 3. **F6 (shells)**: redesigned main dashboard + detail page scaffolding with the new card/hero chrome, using placeholder data. This lets the other features slot into a finished-looking layout. 4. **F1**: dotfiles panel + sync button + backend helpers + sudoers drop-in. 5. **F3**: domain expiry banner + whois install + cached helper. 6. **F2**: openclaw detail page. **First step: run `openclaw status --all --json` on the VM and document the actual JSON shape** before writing the Go struct. Then backend endpoint, then frontend card rendering with the SVG bar chart. 7. **Cleanup**: `apt remove --purge sqlite3` on the VM. Estimated total: ~4.5 hours of focused work. ## Known risks to resolve during implementation - **F2 openclaw status JSON shape is unverified**. Step 1 of F2 is to run the command and inspect output. If `telegram_sessions` isn't exposed, use the fallback plan documented in the F2 section. - **Caddy favicon rewrite** may conflict with existing path handlers. Test `/favicon.ico`, `/terminal/favicon.ico`, `/code/favicon.ico` after deploying — all three should return the attlas SVG. If not, adjust the matcher precedence. - **`openclaw status` subprocess may hang** if the gateway is unresponsive. Wrap the call in a 2-second timeout (`exec.CommandContext` with `context.WithTimeout`). On timeout, return last-known-good cached data or a "temporarily unavailable" state — never block the dashboard. - **Anthropic cost_report API** may return 401 if the admin key is revoked or the billing endpoint is disabled for the org. Handle 4xx responses gracefully and show "billing data unavailable" in the spend card instead of crashing. - **`whois` package** may not be installed on a fresh VM before the first run of base-setup. The domain banner backend call must tolerate `whois: command not found` and return "unknown" severity. ## Deployment After every feature commit on the local laptop: 1. Build the frontend: `cd frontend && npm run build` 2. Commit both the source AND the `dist/` changes to the attlas repo. 3. Push to origin/main. 4. SSH to the VM, pull the attlas repo as `agnostic-user`, rebuild alive-server as `agnostic-user`, restart the service: ```bash sudo -u agnostic-user bash -c "cd /home/agnostic-user/iapetus/attlas && git pull" sudo -u agnostic-user bash -c "cd /home/agnostic-user/iapetus/attlas/base-setup/alive-server && PATH=\$PATH:/usr/local/go/bin go build -o attlas-server ." sudo systemctl restart alive-server ``` 5. For changes that touch Caddy or sudoers: additionally run `sudo systemctl reload caddy` and validate sudoers with `sudo visudo -c`. 6. Verify via the dashboard URL that the change is live. ## Out of scope (explicitly, do not add) - Brave search metrics (no API, no on-disk data). - Sqlite as a service (F5 was dropped). - Token counts on the openclaw spend card (dropped by user decision). - Average-cost-per-day footer on the spend card (dropped by user decision). - Provider quota card on the openclaw detail page (dropped in favor of the simpler 3-card layout). - Detail pages for services other than openclaw in this pass. Other services' `details` links can be disabled or point to a stub. - Reverse-proxy refactor to route all services through alive-server. - Service install/uninstall sudoers scope widening. - Multi-VM Terraform module refactor.

## Current state

**Shipped:**
- **Card-based dashboard redesign**: main dashboard now uses the same `Card / card-row / card-headline / card-grid` primitives as the openclaw detail page. Hero + cloud-spend card (full) + infrastructure card + status card + services card (full). No more mixed "sections + yellow rules" old style.
- **Infrastructure card**: name, machine type, zone, external IP, running status dot, `details` + **`stop vm`** buttons.
- **Stop VM endpoint** (`POST /api/vm/stop`): calls `compute.instances.stop` via metadata-token OAuth, uses the cloud-platform scope already on the SA, returns immediately so the HTTP response completes before alive-server dies. Double-confirmation via `window.confirm()` with a clear warning.
- **Cloud spend card (full width)**: combined GCP + Anthropic MTD, with a two-column breakdown underneath. Errors surface inline.
- **All historical VM names in uptime**: dropped the `protoPayload.resourceName` filter, so the chart now covers `openclaw-vm` (Mar 22 → Apr 8 14:34) **and** `simple-zombie` (Apr 8 14:42 → now) as a single unified timeline. Apr 1-7 correctly show 24h each; Apr 8 shows 20h 33m (up all morning, cycled through the rename); Apr 9 shows 3h 46m; Apr 10 shows 9h 34m and counting.
- **BigQuery billing export setup via Terraform** (`infra/singlevm-setup/billing_export.tf`, already `terraform apply`'d):
  - Enabled APIs: `cloudbilling`, `cloudresourcemanager`, `bigquery`, `logging`, `billingbudgets`
  - Dataset: `petprojects-488115:billing_export` (EU, no expiry)
  - IAM: VM SA has `bigquery.dataViewer` on the dataset + `bigquery.jobUser` on the project
- **Anthropic 100x bug fix + Last-30-days window on openclaw detail** (from earlier): now shows ~$0.52 to match the console.

## One manual step you need to do

Terraform can't actually flip the "send billing data to BigQuery" switch — that action only exists in the Cloud Console. Please do this once:

1. Open https://console.cloud.google.com/billing/
2. Select your billing account
3. In the left nav, click **Billing export**
4. On the **BigQuery export** tab, click **Edit settings** under either "Standard usage cost" or "Detailed usage cost" (standard is enough for the dashboard)
5. Pick project = `petprojects-488115`, dataset = `billing_export`
6. **Save**

After that, data trickles in over the next ~24h. The dashboard card currently shows `waiting for first billing export (up to 24h after enabling in console)`; once the first daily shard lands, it'll flip to the real `$X.YZ` number automatically (5-minute cache).

{"parentUuid":"5eb4d950-cf68-422b-9f67-22d4f63d9098","isSidechain":false,"message":{"model":"claude-opus-4-6","id":"msg_015uGVgv48yyeTfYwx4o3EGP","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"text","text":"All services are active and the error message now reads `waiting for first billing export (up to 24h after enabling in console)` — human-friendly and tells the user exactly what to do.

## Current state

**Shipped:**
- **Card-based dashboard redesign**: main dashboard now uses the same `Card / card-row / card-headline / card-grid` primitives as the openclaw detail page. Hero + cloud-spend card (full) + infrastructure card + status card + services card (full). No more mixed "sections + yellow rules" old style.
- **Infrastructure card**: name, machine type, zone, external IP, running status dot, `details` + **`stop vm`** buttons.
- **Stop VM endpoint** (`POST /api/vm/stop`): calls `compute.instances.stop` via metadata-token OAuth, uses the cloud-platform scope already on the SA, returns immediately so the HTTP response completes before alive-server dies. Double-confirmation via `window.confirm()` with a clear warning.
- **Cloud spend card (full width)**: combined GCP + Anthropic MTD, with a two-column breakdown underneath. Errors surface inline.
- **All historical VM names in uptime**: dropped the `protoPayload.resourceName` filter, so the chart now covers `openclaw-vm` (Mar 22 → Apr 8 14:34) **and** `simple-zombie` (Apr 8 14:42 → now) as a single unified timeline. Apr 1-7 correctly show 24h each; Apr 8 shows 20h 33m (up all morning, cycled through the rename); Apr 9 shows 3h 46m; Apr 10 shows 9h 34m and counting.
- **BigQuery billing export setup via Terraform** (`infra/singlevm-setup/billing_export.tf`, already `terraform apply`'d):
  - Enabled APIs: `cloudbilling`, `cloudresourcemanager`, `bigquery`, `logging`, `billingbudgets`
  - Dataset: `petprojects-488115:billing_export` (EU, no expiry)
  - IAM: VM SA has `bigquery.dataViewer` on the dataset + `bigquery.jobUser` on the project
- **Anthropic 100x bug fix + Last-30-days window on openclaw detail** (from earlier): now shows ~$0.52 to match the console.

## One manual step you need to do

Terraform can't actually flip the "send billing data to BigQuery" switch — that action only exists in the Cloud Console. Please do this once:

1. Open https://console.cloud.google.com/billing/
2. Select your billing account
3. In the left nav, click **Billing export**
4. On the **BigQuery export** tab, click **Edit settings** under either "Standard usage cost" or "Detailed usage cost" (standard is enough for the dashboard)
5. Pick project = `petprojects-488115`, dataset = `billing_export`
6. **Save**

After that, data trickles in over the next ~24h. The dashboard card currently shows `waiting for first billing export (up to 24h after enabling in console)`; once the first daily shard lands, it'll flip to the real `$X.YZ` number automatically (5-minute cache).

content.text: Let me check the diary entry from yesterday and current state of each feature.

content.content:
1→---
     2→title: "Day 3 — Dashboard Refactor, Diary Palette, Live Data"
     3→date: 2026-04-10
     4→summary: "Five dashboard features in one pass: frontend refactor, favicon + titles, dotfiles panel, domain banner, openclaw detail page."
     5→---
     6→
     7→**Claude session:** [`4242bcf4-e708-4fe1-b840-8fea9715ff05`](/diary/sessions/4242bcf4-e708-4fe1-b840-8fea9715ff05.jsonl) (planning) → follow-up execution session
     8→
     9→## Summary
    10→
    11→Took the locked `details-plan.md` from the planning session and
    12→executed all five features end to end: frontend refactor, tab title
    13→and favicon consistency (F0), dotfiles sync panel (F1), openclaw
    14→detail page (F2), domain expiry banner (F3), and the dashboard
    15→redesign (F6). No scope changed from the plan, only discovery and
    16→small mechanical fixes along the way.
    17→
    18→## Timeline
    19→
    20→### Frontend refactor + F6 shell
    21→- `npm install react-router-dom` into `base-setup/alive-server/frontend`.
    22→- Split the monolithic `App.jsx` into a router + `Layout` + `pages/
    23→  Dashboard.jsx` + `pages/detail/Openclaw.jsx`. Extracted
    24→  `StatusDot`, `Card`, `Button` and `Banner` into `src/components/`.
    25→- Centralised `/api/status` fetching behind a `StatusProvider` +
    26→  `useStatus()` context so both pages share the same snapshot.
    27→- Rewrote `index.css` around the diary-derived CSS variables
    28→  (`--bg`, `--fg`, `--border`, `--accent`, `--brand`, `--header`,
    29→  `--surface`, `--danger`, `--warning`). Inter for body text,
    30→  JetBrains Mono reserved for IPs, hashes, paths and timestamps.
    31→- Main dashboard: hero line ("all systems healthy"), consolidated
    32→  STATUS section (vm/ip/domain/claude/dotfiles), SERVICES list with
    33→  `open` / `details` / `×` text actions. Openclaw gets a working
    34→  `details` link; the other three render a greyed-out stub.
    35→
    36→### F0 — favicon + titles
    37→- Hand-drew `frontend/public/favicon.svg` — brand-orange Atlas with
    38→  accent-green highlights on a celestial sphere, hands visibly open
    39→  and apart so he's *not* holding it.
    40→- Set `<title>attlas</title>` + icon link in `index.html`.
    41→- ttyd got `-t titleFixed=attlas`, code-server got `--app-name
    42→  attlas` (both flags verified with `--help` on the VM before
    43→  shipping, per the "test before telling" rule).
    44→- Hugo `baseof.html` title block rewritten to `attlas` /
    45→  `attlas — {{ .Title }}`.
    46→- Caddyfile grew a global `@favicon path_regexp ^/.*favicon\.(ico|
    47→  svg|png)$` matcher that rewrites every subpath's favicon to
    48→  `/favicon.svg` and reverse-proxies to alive-server. First pass
    49→  broke because the global `@protected` matcher redirected every
    50→  non-oauth path to `/oauth2/login`, including favicons; fix was
    51→  to exclude the favicon regex from the `@protected` list. Verified
    52→  after the reload: `/favicon.svg`, `/terminal/favicon.ico`,
    53→  `/code/favicon.ico` and `/diary/favicon.png` all return 200 with
    54→  the same SVG.
    55→
    56→### F1 — dotfiles panel
    57→- New `DotfilesStatus` struct with `last_sync`, exit status,
    58→  head/remote short hashes, commit-behind count and a semantic
    59→  status string.
    60→- `getDotfilesStatus()` reads `ExecMainExitTimestamp` +
    61→  `ExecMainStatus` from `systemctl show dotfiles-sync.service`,
    62→  then shells out to `git -C <dotfiels> rev-parse / log /
    63→  rev-list`. The dotfiels path is derived from `ATTLAS_DIR`'s
    64→  parent so the typo stays single-sourced.
    65→- `POST /api/dotfiles/sync` runs `sudo -n systemctl start
    66→  dotfiles-sync.service`, authorized via a tightly-scoped new
    67→  `/etc/sudoers.d/alive-svc-dotfiles-sync` drop-in.
    68→- Frontend: dotfiles row in the STATUS section with a coloured dot
    69→  (green up-to-date / yellow behind / red error), short hash, "X
    70→  min ago" relative time, and a `sync now` text link.
    71→- First probe came back with blank git fields — alive-svc couldn't
    72→  `git rev-parse` a repo owned by agnostic-user. Fix:
    73→  `git config --system safe.directory` for both dotfiels and attlas
    74→  in `base-setup/setup.sh`, applied by hand on the current VM.
    75→
    76→### F3 — domain expiry banner
    77→- `DomainExpiry` struct with a 6-hour cache; days-remaining still
    78→  ticks down between fetches.
    79→- Shells out to `whois attlas.uk`, regex-matches the Nominet
    80→  `Expiry date: DD-Mon-YYYY` line (the format has leading whitespace
    81→  and two spaces after the colon — the regex is tolerant). Maps
    82→  `ok` > 60d / `warn` 30–60d / `danger` < 30d.
    83→- Tolerates a missing `whois` binary by returning severity
    84→  `unknown` so `/api/status` doesn't crash on a fresh VM.
    85→- Added `whois` to the apt-get install line in `base-setup/setup.sh`
    86→  and installed it by hand on the current box.
    87→- Banner component mounted in the layout above every page. Hidden
    88→  on `ok` and `unknown`, yellow on `warn`, red on `danger`. Current
    89→  reading: `attlas.uk` expires 2027-04-09, 363 days, severity ok,
    90→  so the banner stays hidden.
    91→
    92→### F2 — openclaw detail page
    93→- Ran `sudo -u openclaw-svc -H openclaw status --all --json` on the
    94→  VM as the first step (the plan flagged this as an unknown). The
    95→  shape is: `tasks.total / active / byStatus.running`, `sessions.
    96→  count`, `agents.totalSessions`, plus `gateway.reachable` and
    97→  `gatewayService.runtime.status`. No telegram-specific sessions
    98→  surface because `channelSummary` is empty until the gateway is
    99→  paired, so the usage card ships with `sessions` (label dropped
   100→  the "telegram" qualifier) + `tasks run` (lifetime).
   101→- `GET /api/services/openclaw` with a 30-second cache. Uptime from
   102→  `systemctl show openclaw-gateway -p ActiveEnterTimestamp`.
   103→  Spend data from the Anthropic admin `/v1/organizations/cost_
   104→  report` endpoint, keyed on the new `OAuthConfig.AnthropicAdminKey`
   105→  field (mapped from `anthropic_admin_key` in
   106→  `attlas-server-config`). Gracefully falls back to "billing data
   107→  unavailable" on any 4xx / 5xx / timeout.
   108→- Extended the existing `attlas-server-config` secret (version 3)
   109→  already contained the admin key, but the on-disk
   110→  `/var/lib/alive-server/.attlas-server-config.json` was written
   111→  by the earlier base-setup run and didn't have the new field. Re-
   112→  fetched from Secret Manager by hand and restarted the service.
   113→- Sudoers drop-in: `/etc/sudoers.d/alive-svc-openclaw` keeps HOME
   114→  and grants `alive-svc` the right to run exactly `/usr/bin/
   115→  openclaw` as `openclaw-svc`.
   116→- Frontend: 3-card layout (SPEND with inline SVG bar chart — 7
   117→  `<rect>` bars each carrying a `<title>` child for native tooltips,
   118→  today bar coloured `--brand` — USAGE with sessions/tasks, STATUS
   119→  with running/uptime/active).
   120→
   121→### Cleanup
   122→- `sudo apt remove --purge sqlite3` on the VM — the package was
   123→  installed during discovery when F5 was still on the table, and
   124→  the dropped F5 never reshipped.
   125→
   126→## Final state
   127→
   128→All five services active (`alive-server`, `ttyd`, `code-server`,
   129→`openclaw-gateway`, `caddy`, plus `dotfiles-sync.timer`). The new
   130→`/api/status` carries the full hero-ready payload: VM info, claude
   131→auth status, 4/4 services running, dotfiles up-to-date on
   132→`8c3ea15`, domain expires 2027-04-09 with 363 days remaining.
   133→Openclaw detail endpoint returns uptime, sessions/tasks counters
   134→and a 7-bucket zero-filled spend series (no admin-API spend this
   135→month yet, which is expected).
   136→
   137→## Lessons
   138→
   139→- **Always test flags before shipping them.** The plan said
   140→  `--title-format=attlas` for ttyd and `--app-name=attlas` for
   141→  code-server. ttyd actually uses `-t titleFixed=...` (client
   142→  option), and code-server's flag is `--app-name`. Ran `--help` on
   143→  the VM for both before writing the install-script edits.
   144→- **System-wide `safe.directory` is non-optional** whenever one
   145→  system user queries a repo owned by another. Without
   146→  `/etc/gitconfig` entries, alive-svc's `git rev-parse` over the
   147→  dotfiels tree just silently errors and the UI shows "unknown".
   148→- **Secret Manager config is pull-based, not watch-based.**
   149→  Updating the `attlas-server-config` secret in Secret Manager
   150→  does NOT automatically refresh `/var/lib/alive-server/.attlas-
   151→  server-config.json` — base-setup writes it once and the service
   152→  reads from local disk. Needs either a base-setup re-run, a
   153→  manual `gcloud secrets versions access | tee ...`, or a config-
   154→  reload hook on alive-server. For today it was a manual tee.
   155→- **The backend timeout helper (`runCmdCtx`) is worth it.** Every
   156→  subprocess that touches systemctl, git, whois, openclaw or the
   157→  Anthropic API is now bounded by a hard deadline, so a single
   158→  wedged dependency can't stall `/api/status`.
   159→
   160→## Commits
   161→
   162→1. `Refactor dashboard to router + pages, apply diary palette, wire F0`
   163→2. `Let favicon routes bypass forward_auth in Caddyfile`
   164→3. `Add dotfiles, domain expiry and openclaw detail backend (F1, F3, F2)`
   165→4. `Mark dotfiels/attlas as safe.directory in setup.sh`

---
title: "Day 3 — Dashboard Refactor, Diary Palette, Live Data"
date: 2026-04-10
summary: "Five dashboard features in one pass: frontend refactor, favicon + titles, dotfiles panel, domain banner, openclaw detail page."
---

**Claude session:** [`4242bcf4-e708-4fe1-b840-8fea9715ff05`](/diary/sessions/4242bcf4-e708-4fe1-b840-8fea9715ff05.jsonl) (planning) → follow-up execution session

## Summary

Took the locked `details-plan.md` from the planning session and
executed all five features end to end: frontend refactor, tab title
and favicon consistency (F0), dotfiles sync panel (F1), openclaw
detail page (F2), domain expiry banner (F3), and the dashboard
redesign (F6). No scope changed from the plan, only discovery and
small mechanical fixes along the way.

## Timeline

### Frontend refactor + F6 shell
- `npm install react-router-dom` into `base-setup/alive-server/frontend`.
- Split the monolithic `App.jsx` into a router + `Layout` + `pages/
  Dashboard.jsx` + `pages/detail/Openclaw.jsx`. Extracted
  `StatusDot`, `Card`, `Button` and `Banner` into `src/components/`.
- Centralised `/api/status` fetching behind a `StatusProvider` +
  `useStatus()` context so both pages share the same snapshot.
- Rewrote `index.css` around the diary-derived CSS variables
  (`--bg`, `--fg`, `--border`, `--accent`, `--brand`, `--header`,
  `--surface`, `--danger`, `--warning`). Inter for body text,
  JetBrains Mono reserved for IPs, hashes, paths and timestamps.
- Main dashboard: hero line ("all systems healthy"), consolidated
  STATUS section (vm/ip/domain/claude/dotfiles), SERVICES list with
  `open` / `details` / `×` text actions. Openclaw gets a working
  `details` link; the other three render a greyed-out stub.

### F0 — favicon + titles
- Hand-drew `frontend/public/favicon.svg` — brand-orange Atlas with
  accent-green highlights on a celestial sphere, hands visibly open
  and apart so he's *not* holding it.
- Set `<title>attlas</title>` + icon link in `index.html`.
- ttyd got `-t titleFixed=attlas`, code-server got `--app-name
  attlas` (both flags verified with `--help` on the VM before
  shipping, per the "test before telling" rule).
- Hugo `baseof.html` title block rewritten to `attlas` /
  `attlas — {{ .Title }}`.
- Caddyfile grew a global `@favicon path_regexp ^/.*favicon\.(ico|
  svg|png)$` matcher that rewrites every subpath's favicon to
  `/favicon.svg` and reverse-proxies to alive-server. First pass
  broke because the global `@protected` matcher redirected every
  non-oauth path to `/oauth2/login`, including favicons; fix was
  to exclude the favicon regex from the `@protected` list. Verified
  after the reload: `/favicon.svg`, `/terminal/favicon.ico`,
  `/code/favicon.ico` and `/diary/favicon.png` all return 200 with
  the same SVG.

### F1 — dotfiles panel
- New `DotfilesStatus` struct with `last_sync`, exit status,
  head/remote short hashes, commit-behind count and a semantic
  status string.
- `getDotfilesStatus()` reads `ExecMainExitTimestamp` +
  `ExecMainStatus` from `systemctl show dotfiles-sync.service`,
  then shells out to `git -C <dotfiels> rev-parse / log /
  rev-list`. The dotfiels path is derived from `ATTLAS_DIR`'s
  parent so the typo stays single-sourced.
- `POST /api/dotfiles/sync` runs `sudo -n systemctl start
  dotfiles-sync.service`, authorized via a tightly-scoped new
  `/etc/sudoers.d/alive-svc-dotfiles-sync` drop-in.
- Frontend: dotfiles row in the STATUS section with a coloured dot
  (green up-to-date / yellow behind / red error), short hash, "X
  min ago" relative time, and a `sync now` text link.
- First probe came back with blank git fields — alive-svc couldn't
  `git rev-parse` a repo owned by agnostic-user. Fix:
  `git config --system safe.directory` for both dotfiels and attlas
  in `base-setup/setup.sh`, applied by hand on the current VM.

### F3 — domain expiry banner
- `DomainExpiry` struct with a 6-hour cache; days-remaining still
  ticks down between fetches.
- Shells out to `whois attlas.uk`, regex-matches the Nominet
  `Expiry date: DD-Mon-YYYY` line (the format has leading whitespace
  and two spaces after the colon — the regex is tolerant). Maps
  `ok` > 60d / `warn` 30–60d / `danger` < 30d.
- Tolerates a missing `whois` binary by returning severity
  `unknown` so `/api/status` doesn't crash on a fresh VM.
- Added `whois` to the apt-get install line in `base-setup/setup.sh`
  and installed it by hand on the current box.
- Banner component mounted in the layout above every page. Hidden
  on `ok` and `unknown`, yellow on `warn`, red on `danger`. Current
  reading: `attlas.uk` expires 2027-04-09, 363 days, severity ok,
  so the banner stays hidden.

### F2 — openclaw detail page
- Ran `sudo -u openclaw-svc -H openclaw status --all --json` on the
  VM as the first step (the plan flagged this as an unknown). The
  shape is: `tasks.total / active / byStatus.running`, `sessions.
  count`, `agents.totalSessions`, plus `gateway.reachable` and
  `gatewayService.runtime.status`. No telegram-specific sessions
  surface because `channelSummary` is empty until the gateway is
  paired, so the usage card ships with `sessions` (label dropped
  the "telegram" qualifier) + `tasks run` (lifetime).
- `GET /api/services/openclaw` with a 30-second cache. Uptime from
  `systemctl show openclaw-gateway -p ActiveEnterTimestamp`.
  Spend data from the Anthropic admin `/v1/organizations/cost_
  report` endpoint, keyed on the new `OAuthConfig.AnthropicAdminKey`
  field (mapped from `anthropic_admin_key` in
  `attlas-server-config`). Gracefully falls back to "billing data
  unavailable" on any 4xx / 5xx / timeout.
- Extended the existing `attlas-server-config` secret (version 3)
  already contained the admin key, but the on-disk
  `/var/lib/alive-server/.attlas-server-config.json` was written
  by the earlier base-setup run and didn't have the new field. Re-
  fetched from Secret Manager by hand and restarted the service.
- Sudoers drop-in: `/etc/sudoers.d/alive-svc-openclaw` keeps HOME
  and grants `alive-svc` the right to run exactly `/usr/bin/
  openclaw` as `openclaw-svc`.
- Frontend: 3-card layout (SPEND with inline SVG bar chart — 7
  `<rect>` bars each carrying a `<title>` child for native tooltips,
  today bar coloured `--brand` — USAGE with sessions/tasks, STATUS
  with running/uptime/active).

### Cleanup
- `sudo apt remove --purge sqlite3` on the VM — the package was
  installed during discovery when F5 was still on the table, and
  the dropped F5 never reshipped.

## Final state

All five services active (`alive-server`, `ttyd`, `code-server`,
`openclaw-gateway`, `caddy`, plus `dotfiles-sync.timer`). The new
`/api/status` carries the full hero-ready payload: VM info, claude
auth status, 4/4 services running, dotfiles up-to-date on
`8c3ea15`, domain expires 2027-04-09 with 363 days remaining.
Openclaw detail endpoint returns uptime, sessions/tasks counters
and a 7-bucket zero-filled spend series (no admin-API spend this
month yet, which is expected).

## Lessons

- **Always test flags before shipping them.** The plan said
  `--title-format=attlas` for ttyd and `--app-name=attlas` for
  code-server. ttyd actually uses `-t titleFixed=...` (client
  option), and code-server's flag is `--app-name`. Ran `--help` on
  the VM for both before writing the install-script edits.
- **System-wide `safe.directory` is non-optional** whenever one
  system user queries a repo owned by another. Without
  `/etc/gitconfig` entries, alive-svc's `git rev-parse` over the
  dotfiels tree just silently errors and the UI shows "unknown".
- **Secret Manager config is pull-based, not watch-based.**
  Updating the `attlas-server-config` secret in Secret Manager
  does NOT automatically refresh `/var/lib/alive-server/.attlas-
  server-config.json` — base-setup writes it once and the service
  reads from local disk. Needs either a base-setup re-run, a
  manual `gcloud secrets versions access | tee ...`, or a config-
  reload hook on alive-server. For today it was a manual tee.
- **The backend timeout helper (`runCmdCtx`) is worth it.** Every
  subprocess that touches systemctl, git, whois, openclaw or the
  Anthropic API is now bounded by a hard deadline, so a single
  wedged dependency can't stall `/api/status`.

## Commits

1. `Refactor dashboard to router + pages, apply diary palette, wire F0`
2. `Let favicon routes bypass forward_auth in Caddyfile`
3. `Add dotfiles, domain expiry and openclaw detail backend (F1, F3, F2)`
4. `Mark dotfiels/attlas as safe.directory in setup.sh`

1→# Cloud Billing BigQuery export setup.
     2→#
     3→# Terraform can not actually FLIP THE SWITCH that sends billing data
     4→# to BigQuery — that's a one-time manual action in the Cloud Console
     5→# (Billing → Billing export → BigQuery export). What Terraform CAN
     6→# do is stand up everything the export needs on the landing side:
     7→# the APIs it depends on, the dataset the export writes into, and
     8→# the IAM bindings that let the VM's service account read from it.
     9→#
    10→# After `terraform apply`, open the Cloud Console once and point the
    11→# billing account at the `billing_export` dataset in this project.
    12→# From then on the export is populated daily, and the dashboard's
    13→# /api/cloud-spend endpoint queries it directly.
    14→
    15→# 1. Enable the APIs the rest of this file depends on.
    16→#
    17→# `cloudbilling` is the admin surface used by gcloud/console pages.
    18→# `cloudresourcemanager` is needed for project-level IAM bindings.
    19→# `bigquery` is the dataset + query API we actually hit at runtime.
    20→# `logging` powers the daily-uptime replay over audit events.
    21→# `billingbudgets` is optional but cheap — enables future use of
    22→# the Budgets API if we ever want spend alerts on the dashboard.
    23→resource "google_project_service" "enabled" {
    24→  for_each = toset([
    25→    "cloudbilling.googleapis.com",
    26→    "cloudresourcemanager.googleapis.com",
    27→    "bigquery.googleapis.com",
    28→    "logging.googleapis.com",
    29→    "billingbudgets.googleapis.com",
    30→  ])
    31→  project            = var.project_id
    32→  service            = each.value
    33→  disable_on_destroy = false
    34→}
    35→
    36→# 2. The dataset that the Cloud Billing export writes into.
    37→#
    38→# Location is pinned to EU so query egress stays free within the same
    39→# region as the VM. Default table expiration is left unset (null) so
    40→# the export tables live forever — billing history is useful even
    41→# for old months.
    42→resource "google_bigquery_dataset" "billing_export" {
    43→  dataset_id                  = "billing_export"
    44→  friendly_name               = "Cloud Billing BigQuery export"
    45→  description                 = "Destination for Cloud Billing daily cost export. Manual console step wires the billing account to this dataset."
    46→  location                    = "EU"
    47→  default_table_expiration_ms = null
    48→
    49→  depends_on = [google_project_service.enabled]
    50→}
    51→
    52→# 3. Grant the VM's service account read access on the dataset plus
    53→#    the project-level BigQuery Job User role it needs to actually
    54→#    run SELECT queries.
    55→resource "google_bigquery_dataset_iam_member" "vm_data_viewer" {
    56→  dataset_id = google_bigquery_dataset.billing_export.dataset_id
    57→  role       = "roles/bigquery.dataViewer"
    58→  member     = local.vm_service_account
    59→}
    60→
    61→resource "google_project_iam_member" "vm_bq_job_user" {
    62→  project = var.project_id
    63→  role    = "roles/bigquery.jobUser"
    64→  member  = local.vm_service_account
    65→}
    66→

file.content:
# Cloud Billing BigQuery export setup.
#
# Terraform can not actually FLIP THE SWITCH that sends billing data
# to BigQuery — that's a one-time manual action in the Cloud Console
# (Billing → Billing export → BigQuery export). What Terraform CAN
# do is stand up everything the export needs on the landing side:
# the APIs it depends on, the dataset the export writes into, and
# the IAM bindings that let the VM's service account read from it.
#
# After `terraform apply`, open the Cloud Console once and point the
# billing account at the `billing_export` dataset in this project.
# From then on the export is populated daily, and the dashboard's
# /api/cloud-spend endpoint queries it directly.

# 1. Enable the APIs the rest of this file depends on.
#
# `cloudbilling` is the admin surface used by gcloud/console pages.
# `cloudresourcemanager` is needed for project-level IAM bindings.
# `bigquery` is the dataset + query API we actually hit at runtime.
# `logging` powers the daily-uptime replay over audit events.
# `billingbudgets` is optional but cheap — enables future use of
# the Budgets API if we ever want spend alerts on the dashboard.
resource "google_project_service" "enabled" {
  for_each = toset([
    "cloudbilling.googleapis.com",
    "cloudresourcemanager.googleapis.com",
    "bigquery.googleapis.com",
    "logging.googleapis.com",
    "billingbudgets.googleapis.com",
  ])
  project            = var.project_id
  service            = each.value
  disable_on_destroy = false
}

# 2. The dataset that the Cloud Billing export writes into.
#
# Location is pinned to EU so query egress stays free within the same
# region as the VM. Default table expiration is left unset (null) so
# the export tables live forever — billing history is useful even
# for old months.
resource "google_bigquery_dataset" "billing_export" {
  dataset_id                  = "billing_export"
  friendly_name               = "Cloud Billing BigQuery export"
  description                 = "Destination for Cloud Billing daily cost export. Manual console step wires the billing account to this dataset."
  location                    = "EU"
  default_table_expiration_ms = null

  depends_on = [google_project_service.enabled]
}

# 3. Grant the VM's service account read access on the dataset plus
#    the project-level BigQuery Job User role it needs to actually
#    run SELECT queries.
resource "google_bigquery_dataset_iam_member" "vm_data_viewer" {
  dataset_id = google_bigquery_dataset.billing_export.dataset_id
  role       = "roles/bigquery.dataViewer"
  member     = local.vm_service_account
}

resource "google_project_iam_member" "vm_bq_job_user" {
  project = var.project_id
  role    = "roles/bigquery.jobUser"
  member  = local.vm_service_account
}

content.text:
I have a complete picture now. Here's where the dead session stopped:

**Context of the dead session:**
- You loaded `details-plan.md` and executed all 5 features (F0 favicon/titles, F1 dotfiles panel, F2 openclaw detail, F3 domain banner, F6 dashboard redesign)
- Then went beyond the plan: card-based dashboard refactor, Infrastructure detail page, Stop VM endpoint, combined GCP+Anthropic cloud spend card, unified VM uptime across rename, BigQuery billing export Terraform + IAM
- The session died on your last message: **"what is mtd ?"** (interrupted, then "hi", then it couldn't be resumed)

**Open points to close:**
1. Answer "what is MTD?"
2. Update the diary entry — it was written at 20:51 covering only the plan items, but the Infrastructure card / Stop VM / billing export / VM uptime unification / 100x bug fix came **after** the diary was saved
3. Delete `details-plan.md` (you just asked)
4. Decide on the dangling question: enable Anthropic billing export too, or leave cost_report API?

Let me answer the MTD question first, then do the work.

**MTD = Month-to-Date.** It's the total spend from the 1st of the current month through today. The cloud-spend card reads `anthropic_mtd_usd` + `gcp_mtd_usd` + `total_mtd_usd` — each is the running sum for April 1 → today. So "Anthropic MTD from cost_report" just means "the running April total pulled from Anthropic's cost_report API" (as opposed to pulling it from a BigQuery billing export).

On the dangling question: **keep the Anthropic cost_report API, don't also enable an Anthropic billing export.** Reasons: (a) Anthropic doesn't have a BigQuery-style billing export — `cost_report` is the canonical source, (b) it already works and returns correct MTD after the 100x bug fix, (c) duplicating it via some scraping path just adds failure modes for no benefit. GCP gets the BigQuery export because there is no simple MTD API for it.

Now let me update the diary and delete the plan.
